stem: Which personal financial statement presents a client's financial position at a specific point in time?

options.B: The balance sheet (statement of net worth)

explanation: 教材 1.1 与 2.1 指出资产负债表(净值表)反映某一特定时点的财务现状;收入表才是反映一段期间收入与支出的报表。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.23 — s.1.1, s.2.1

stem: George's total assets are S$310,000 and his total liabilities are S$131,000. What is his net worth?
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.23 — s.2.5

stem: Which of the following is the BEST example of a liquid asset?

options.B: The home a client lives in
options.C: A unit trust held for retirement
options.D: An antique car held for capital gains

explanation: 教材 2.2(A1)将流动资产定义为现金及可在一年内轻易变现的近现金资源,如储蓄/支票账户、定期存款与货币市场工具。自住房属个人资产;为退休持有的单位信托属投资资产;意在增值变卖的古董车属投资资产。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.23 — s.2.2 (A1)

stem: A client has a regular, stable monthly income. According to the guideline in the chapter, how many months' worth of expenses should generally be set aside as emergency funds?
explanation: 教材 2.2(A1)建议至少预留 3 至 6 个月开支作应急基金;只有当收入不规律时才建议预留相当于 12 个月开支的应急基金,故 D 适用于不规律收入情形。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.23 — s.2.2 (A1)

stem: On a personal balance sheet, how are liabilities such as a home mortgage normally reported?

options.B: At the higher of cost and market value
options.C: At original cost, showing the outstanding principal
options.D: At the total of principal plus all future interest

explanation: 教材 2.4:负债通常按原始成本报告,显示本金金额。例:S$200,000 房贷,月供 S$3,000 中 S$1,000 抵减本金,还款后列示 S$199,000。利息不计入所报负债余额。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.23 — s.2.4

stem: George's total income for 2023 was S$70,000 and his total expenditure was S$76,000. His cash flow for the year is therefore:
explanation: 教材 3.3:净现金流 = 总流入 − 总流出 = 70,000 − 76,000 = (S$6,000),即赤字(流出大于流入)。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.23 — s.3.1–3.3

stem: The liquidity ratio is calculated as liquid assets divided by monthly expenses. Using George's figures (liquid assets S$10,000; total annual expenditure S$76,000), his liquidity ratio is approximately:
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.23 — s.4.1–4.2

stem: Which statement about the savings ratio is correct, given George saves & invests S$13,000 out of total income of S$70,000?

options.A: His savings ratio is 18.6% and falls short of the 10% guideline
options.B: His savings ratio is 18.6% and meets the 10% guideline
options.C: His savings ratio is 10.0% and exactly meets the guideline
options.D: Investments are excluded, so his savings ratio is 0%

explanation: 教材 4.5:储蓄比率 = 储蓄与投资 ÷ 总收入 = 13,000 ÷ 70,000 = 18.6%,高于 10% 指引,故 George 储蓄/投资充足。注意储蓄包含投资,故 D 错。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.23 — s.4.5

stem: George paid S$12,000 in mortgage and S$10,000 in car loan instalments during the year, and his annual take-home pay is S$50,000. His debt service ratio is 44%. How does this compare with the guideline?

options.A: It is favourable, being below the 50% guideline
options.B: It is favourable, being below the 15% guideline
options.C: It is exactly at the 35% guideline
options.D: It is unfavourable, exceeding the 35% guideline

explanation: 教材 4.7:偿债比率 = 年贷款还款(12,000+10,000=22,000) ÷ 年实得薪酬(50,000) = 44%,高于 35% 指引,属不利,表明 George 用过多实得薪酬还贷。50% 是债务对资产比率的指引,15% 是流动资产对净值比率的指引,均不适用于偿债比率。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.23 — s.4.7

stem: The debt-to-asset ratio shows how much of a person's assets are financed by debt. With total liabilities S$131,000 and total assets S$310,000, George's ratio of 42.3% means:

options.A: For every dollar of assets, about 42.3 cents was borrowed, comparing favourably with the 50% guideline
options.B: For every dollar of assets, about 42.3 cents truly belongs to him
options.C: He is technically insolvent because the ratio exceeds 35%
options.D: He should reduce his savings to lower the ratio

explanation: 教材 4.6:债务对资产比率 = 131,000 ÷ 310,000 = 42.3%,意指每 1 元资产中 42.3 分靠借贷,越低越好,优于 50% 指引。B 描述的是偿付能力比率;资不抵债指负债超过资产(净值为负),与 35% 无关。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.23 — s.4.6

stem: George's solvency ratio (net worth ÷ total assets) is 57.7%. What does this MOST accurately indicate?

options.A: 57.7% of his income is saved each year
options.B: He can cover 57.7 months of expenses from liquid assets
options.C: His liabilities exceed his assets, making him insolvent
options.D: For every dollar of assets, 57.7 cents truly belong to him after settling all debts

explanation: 教材 4.10:偿付能力比率 = 净值 ÷ 总资产 = 179,000 ÷ 310,000 = 57.7%,表示每 1 元资产中 57.7 分在清偿全部债务后真正属于他。C 错:负债超过资产、净值为负才是资不抵债,George 净值为正。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.23 — s.4.10

stem: Which statement BEST reflects the chapter's view on using personal financial ratios?

options.A: A single ratio, such as the solvency ratio, is sufficient to judge financial health
options.B: No single ratio tells the whole story; several ratios must be viewed together against guidelines
options.C: Ratios replace the need for a balance sheet and income statement
options.D: Ratios are only useful for businesses, not individuals

explanation: 教材 4.1:比率分析从财务报表提取数据生成关键比率,但没有单一比率能说明全貌,需综合多个比率并与建议指引值对照,才能形成对个人财务健康的整体判断。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.23 — s.4.1
